subject: lenny-m0l
message: E2E (live iPad): verify text-entry goal completes on-device — confirms lenny-c94/whn/22f

bead.title: E2E (live iPad): verify text-entry goal completes on-device — confirms lenny-c94/whn/22f
bead.description: The three text-entry fixes are MERGED to main (lenny-c94 widget-tree enter_text @ 2c95c8a, lenny-whn semantics captureAsync @ ac4f82e, lenny-22f DPR coordinate fix @ 053bce5) but have NOT been verified on a real device. Run the live-device dogfood E2E for a text-entry goal and confirm the agent can now type into the login form and complete the goal end-to-end. Procedure: docs/RUNBOOK-e2e-dogfood.md (section 5, Text-entry scenario). This is a MANUAL on-device run (wired iPad), not an automated test and not a factory/code bead.
bead.acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On the wired iPad, an agent run with a text-entry goal shows core.enter_text executed against the Semantics(textField:true) wrapper node with result.ok:true (previously failed with 'target_unreachable: ... does not advertise SemanticsAction.setText')
- [ ] The goal completes end-to-end: footer.outcome == done and routeStack reaches the home screen
- [ ] The trajectory (packages/exploration_cli/trajectories/*.jsonl) is captured/noted for the record
bead.notes: Run per docs/RUNBOOK-e2e-dogfood.md. Setup: wired iPad (uncheck 'Connect via network' in Xcode); build the app from main (carries all 3 fixes); run the agent CLI from main; source ~/.lenny-dogfood.env. CAVEAT: the login fields may be pre-filled with valid creds, so phrase the goal to force typing (e.g. 'change the email to user@example.com, then sign in') and verify the executed core.enter_text action, not just final completion. Execution deferred to a future session per user. Related closed beads: lenny-c94, lenny-whn, lenny-22f. Next blockers after this: lenny-18q (go_router navigate).
bead.close_reason: On-device text-entry E2E PASS (wired iPad mini, DPR=2): outcome=done, enter_text ok:true on email+password wrapper nodes, routeStack=home. Required fix lenny-8k3 (now merged to main, 721308e). AC1/AC2/AC3 met. Trajectory 20260607T175603Z.jsonl.

subject: lenny-18q
message: [bug] router.navigate broken for MaterialApp.router apps (uses pushNamed/onGenerateRoute)

bead.title: [bug] router.navigate broken for MaterialApp.router apps (uses pushNamed/onGenerateRoute)
bead.description: RouterPlugin._NavigateTool.call (packages/exploration_router/lib/src/router_plugin.dart) does state.pushNamed(routeName) — the Navigator 1.0 named-route API, which requires Navigator.onGenerateRoute. The sample app (and most modern Flutter apps) use MaterialApp.router with a routerConfig (Router API / declarative routing) where onGenerateRoute is null. Every router.navigate call fails: 'unknown route "X": Navigator.onGenerateRoute was null'. Observed across a full live agent run (lenny-jfh re-run, 2026-05-22): turns 3-49 the agent tried ~20 route-name variants, all failed identically. RouterPlugin must navigate via the routerDelegate / a declarative route change, not pushNamed, when the app uses the Router API.

bead.close_reason: Fixed via PR #45 (squash 5177486 on main): RouterPlugin navigation seam; sample_app wires go_router goNamed; pushNamed retained as Navigator-1.0 fallback. On-device PASS: navigate{settings} ok:true -> observation current_route_name='settings' -> done. 14/14 router tests, CI green.

subject: lenny-jfh
message: Agent cannot complete state-change goals: riverpod plugin is invalidate-only + agent loops instead of switching strategy

bead.title: Agent cannot complete state-change goals: riverpod plugin is invalidate-only + agent loops instead of switching strategy
bead.description:
Observed in the first full live Claude run (50 turns, goal 'turn on dark mode', after lenny-966 + lenny-jox diagnosis). The agent worked correctly end-to-end -- connected, observed the live app, reasoned that the goal needs the 'settings' provider's theme set to dark -- and made ~38 valid tool calls. But it could NOT complete the goal and ended outcome=budget_exhausted (50-turn cap).

TWO intertwined gaps:
1. The riverpod plugin only exposes 'riverpod.invalidate_provider' -- which RESETS a provider to its initial state. There is no set-state / mutate tool. So the agent literally cannot set themeMode=dark via riverpod. It tried many arg shapes ({patch:{theme:dark}}, {update:{themeMode:dark}}, {action:set,key:darkMode,value:true}) -- all ignored because invalidate_provider takes no such args.
2. The agent did not switch strategy. It called invalidate_provider({provider:settings}) ~38 times in a row without progress, never trying the UI path (it never logged in, navigated to a settings screen, or tapped a dark-mode toggle) and never calling core.done. A loop/no-progress detector or a prompt nudge should make it abandon an ineffective tool.

Fix direction (needs triage / product decision): either add a riverpod state-mutation tool, and/or ensure the agent takes the UI path for UI-reachable goals, and/or add no-progress detection so it changes approach. Filed as a finding from the first successful live run; the agent INFRASTRUCTURE works -- this is a capability/behavior gap.
